en.extract:
The pygmy heleia (Heleia squamifrons), also known as the Bornean ibon, pygmy ibon, and pygmy white-eye, is a species of bird in the white-eye family Zosteropidae.


Description
The pygmy heleia possesses a gray-brown and yellow coloring, along with a white-speckled forehead that has been described as 'scaly'.


Distribution and habitat
It is endemic to the hill forest and lower montane forest of northern Borneo.


Behaviour
It feeds on small berries, fruits, seeds and insects, foraging in small flocks of 4 to 8 birds. It will associate with other birds when feeding, including other white-eyes, cuckoo-doves, yuhinas and erpornises. The species is common but inconspicuous. Its calls are high-pitched.


References

va Balen, Bas (2008), "Family Zosteropidae (White-eyes)", in Josep, del Hoyo; Andrew, Elliott; David, Christie (eds.), Handbook of the Birds of the World. Volume 13, Penduline-tits to Shrikes, Barcelona: Lynx Edicions, p. 483, ISBN 978-84-96553-45-3

The '''pygmy heleia''' ('''''Heleia squamifrons'''''), also known as the '''Bornean ibon<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Pygmy Heleia - eBird |url=https://ebird.org/species/pyweye1 |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20250908064441/https://ebird.org/species/pyweye1 |archive-date=2025-09-08 |access-date=2026-04-17 |website=ebird.org |language=en}}</ref>''', '''pygmy ibon''', and '''pygmy white-eye''',<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/ioc-lists/master-list-2/ |title=Master Lists: v15.1 |website=[[IOC World Bird List]] |publisher=[[International Ornithologists' Union]] |access-date=13 March 2025}}</ref> is a species of [[bird]] in the white-eye family [[Zosteropidae]].

== Description ==
The pygmy heleia possesses a gray-brown and yellow coloring, along with a white-speckled forehead that has been described as 'scaly'.<ref name=":0" />

==Distribution and habitat==
It is [[endemism|endemic]] to the hill forest and lower montane forest of northern [[Borneo]].<ref>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Quentin |author2=  Phillipps, Karen |name-list-style= amp |year=2011 |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 }}</ref>

==Behaviour==
It feeds on small berries, fruits, seeds and insects, foraging in small flocks of 4 to 8 birds. It will associate with other birds when feeding, including other white-eyes, cuckoo-doves, [[yuhina]]s and [[erpornis]]es. The species is common but inconspicuous. Its calls are high-pitched.<ref name=":0" />
